overview: Jeskai Control is an Azorius Control variant that splashes red to effectively handle the creature-heavy Standard meta. The deck aims to control the board using spot removal, counterspells, and lifegain until the opponent exhausts their resources. Once the board is stabilized, it transitions to powerful win conditions to close out the game.

keep_criteria: Versatile hands that perform well across multiple matchups
mulligan_criteria: Hands lacking interaction or land drops
Risky hands against unknown opponents that rely on topdeck manipulation

examples.reason: Has ways to interact with the opponent but needs land drops to put plans in motion.
Can perform really well in many matchups and is versatile enough to keep in most cases.

early_game: Use spot removal and counterspells to control the board
Gain life to survive aggro strategies
mid_game: Continue controlling the board and preventing opponent value
Bounce problematic creatures with Marang River Regent
Manage resources and keep backup plans in hand
late_game: Deploy win conditions like Elspeth, Storm Slayer and Beza, the Bounding Spring
Finish the game once the opponent runs out of resources

key_cards.name: Elspeth, Storm Slayer
key_cards.notes: Fills board with tokens, buffs creatures with evasion, and removes 3+ cost creatures.

key_cards.name: Beza, the Bounding Spring
key_cards.notes: Deals with aggro decks, interacts well with Elspeth, and finishes games.

key_cards.name: Marang River Regent
key_cards.notes: Excellent in combat, creates card advantage, and bounces creatures.

key_cards.name: Ride's End
key_cards.notes: Deals with recursive creatures like Mosswood Dreadknight.

key_cards.name: Tishana's Tidebinder
key_cards.notes: Versatile ability lets you block many meta cards.

key_cards.name: Regal Caracal
key_cards.notes: One of the best cards against aggro, complements Beza.

sequencing_tips: Don't pass turns without mana open against bounce strategies.
Keep global removals in hand for the right timing.
Control your opponent's board and life points carefully against aggro.
Prevent opponents from creating value with creatures, especially when Cauldron is in play.
Use removals wisely and save them for the right time.

matchups.advice: Control the opponent and prevent them from creating value with creatures, especially if Cauldron is in play.
Control the board and prevent value from problematic cards; Jeskai has the upper hand in game 1.
Play around the opponent's sequences and avoid passing turns without mana.
Control the board and life points carefully, using removals at the right time.
Disrupt the combo and game plan in any way possible until you can deploy your win conditions.
watch_for: damage when artifacts leave the board

overview_evidence.evidence_span: Jeskai Control is an Azorius Control variant that splashes to red to take advantage of how valuable this color is in many matchups. ... The main game plan is simple: all you have to do is control the game until your opponent runs out of resources and you can easily play your win condition.

win_conditions_evidence.evidence_span: Elspeth, Storm Slayer is that win condition.
It is also a great finisher.

sequencing_tips_evidence.evidence_span: don't pass turns without any mana because this could be fatal.
Keep a few global removals for the right time, and use them wisely.
don't give your opponent even an inch of freedom, and always have a backup plan in hand.

common_threats_evidence.evidence_span: Vivi Ornitier is still dominating Magic: The Gathering.
Be careful with Kaito, Bane of Nightmares and his Ninjutsu.
Be careful with Screaming Nemesis because it can be very problematic for you
Ride's End deals with recursive creatures (like Mosswood Dreadknight) really well.
Basically, your opponent will use Weapons Manufacturing to fill their board with artifacts.
